title: Simple & Compound Interest
learning_outcomes: Describe structural characteristics of Simple & Compound Interest.
Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for SI vs CI formulas derivations, CI compounding periods (semi-annual, quarterly), CI installment plans calculations.

content_sections.title: Simple & Compound Interest
content_sections.content:
### Arithmetic, Totients & Combinatorics
Quantitative Aptitude focuses on number relationships, algebraic systems, and probability calculations.

#### Euler's Totient Theorem
For coprimes $a$ and $n$, the remainder of $a^{\phi(n)} \pmod n$ is $1$, where $\phi(n)$ is Euler's totient (count of numbers coprime to $n$ up to $n$):
$$\phi(n) = n \prod_{p \mid n} \left(1 - \frac{1}{p}\right)$$
For primes, $\phi(p) = p - 1$, yielding Fermat's Little Theorem: $a^{p-1} \equiv 1 \pmod p$.

#### Product Consistency Constraint
If $A \times B = C$ and $C$ remains constant, a fractional increase in $A$ by $x/y$ requires a fractional decrease in $B$ by $\frac{x}{x + y}$ to maintain product consistency.

#### Stars-and-Bars Partitions
To distribute $n$ identical objects among $k$ distinct bins:
*   *Empty bins allowed:* $\binom{n + k - 1}{k - 1}$ partitions.
*   *No empty bins allowed:* $\binom{n - 1}{k - 1}$ partitions.

### Depth Analysis: Simple & Compound Interest
In production configurations, we analyze how SI vs CI formulas derivations, CI compounding periods (semi-annual, quarterly), CI installment plans calculations affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Simple & Compound Interest
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Simple & Compound Interest is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Simple & Compound Interest
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Simple & Compound Interest loops.
